title: Code hotfix with implementer/tester separation of duties
summary: Drives a medium-risk code defect from a failing reproduction through a fix, an independent verification, and a successful close. The tester role must be a different actor than the implementer (SoD).

steps.summary: Supervisor creates the workflow task pinned to hotfix_fastlane@1, binds implementer + tester, sets risk=medium.
Implementer applies the start transition (open -> active, still in red).

evidence.summary: Same case now passes after fix

steps.summary: Implementer transitions red -> green. Effects fire: declare_handoff(toRole=tester) + wake_role_session(role=tester) because risk>=medium and tester is bound.
Tester attaches verification_report after replay on the build under test.

evidence.summary: Replay clean against build-2026-05-08T22:14Z; smoke green

steps.summary: Tester transitions green -> verified. SoD requires acting actor != implementer binding.
Implementer closes the task as success.

negativeChecks.name: implementer-actor-cannot-act-as-tester
negativeChecks.summary: After step 05, attempt to apply 'verify' with actor=clod claiming role=tester. Since clod is not bound to the tester role, expect rejection code 'role_not_bound'. The kernel rejects role-binding mismatch before evaluating SoD.

negativeChecks.name: sod-same-actor-both-roles
negativeChecks.summary: Create a separate task where the SAME actor is bound to both implementer AND tester (e.g. roleBindings.implementer=clod, roleBindings.tester=clod). After reaching green phase, apply 'verify' as tester. Expect rejection code 'sod_violation' because mustDifferFrom check fires.
negativeChecks.note: Requires a separate task creation with same-actor bindings to reach the SoD check path.

negativeChecks.name: missing-evidence-blocks-fix
negativeChecks.summary: If step 04 is skipped, applying 'implement_fix' must reject with 'missing_evidence' listing commit_ref and regression_test.
